findings.title: Deprecated Apache Kafka Migration Risk
findings.description: The current use of Apache Kafka 2.8, which is reaching end-of-life in Q1 2026, poses a critical risk due to known vulnerabilities and incompatibility with the new KRaft consensus protocol. This migration requires significant effort to update all producer and consumer services.
assumptions: All services must be updated to ensure compatibility with Kafka 3.7+

evidence.excerpt: Migrate from deprecated Apache Kafka 2.x to Kafka 3.7+

recommendations: Prioritize migration to Kafka 3.7+ in the MVP.

findings.title: Vendor Lock-in on Proprietary Transformation Engine
findings.description: The reliance on VendorX's proprietary ETL SDK creates a dependency that risks increased costs and potential service disruption. Evaluating open-source alternatives is essential to eliminate this risk.
assumptions: Open-source alternatives can meet current functionality requirements.

evidence.excerpt: Eliminate vendor lock-in on proprietary transformation engine

recommendations: Evaluate open-source alternatives and plan migration in V1.

findings.title: Python 3.9 EOL Migration Risk
findings.description: The need to upgrade from Python 3.9 to 3.12 across 8 services poses a risk due to compatibility issues with existing dependencies, particularly numpy and custom C extensions.
assumptions: All services can be upgraded without major rewrites.

evidence.excerpt: Python 3.9 EOL migration for all pipeline workers

recommendations: Plan for Python migration in V1, addressing dependency issues.

findings.title: Redis Cluster Migration Dependency
findings.description: The current single-region Redis cluster needs to be replaced with a multi-region solution to address latency issues and prepare for Redis 6 EOL in March 2026. This requires evaluating Redis 7+ or alternative caching solutions.
assumptions: Multi-region caching can be implemented without significant architectural changes.

evidence.excerpt: Replace single-region Redis cluster with multi-region caching

recommendations: Evaluate Redis 7+ and alternative solutions for V2.

findings.title: Dependency Health Dashboard Implementation
findings.description: Creating a centralized dependency health dashboard is essential for proactive management of EOL and CVE issues across the platform. This will improve visibility and alerting for dependency-related risks.
assumptions: Integration with GitHub Dependabot and Snyk is feasible.

evidence.excerpt: Create dependency health dashboard and automated CVE alerting

recommendations: Implement the dashboard in the MVP to enhance visibility.

summary: The analysis reveals critical risks associated with deprecated technologies and vendor lock-in, necessitating immediate migration efforts. A phased delivery plan is proposed, prioritizing the migration of Kafka and Python in the MVP, followed by addressing vendor lock-in and caching solutions in subsequent versions.
